stories.headline: BTC slides under $77K, ETH below $2,300 ahead of Fed as $2.65T crypto market sheds 2.3%
stories.tldr: Bitcoin pulled back to $76,472 and Ether to $2,278 as traders de-risked into Wednesday's FOMC, lopping 2.3% off total crypto market cap.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A hawkish hold while Brent trades above $104 would extend the breakdown from $80K, with analysts flagging $74,182 as the next bid zone before structure breaks.
the_details: BTC opened at $77,368 and printed $76,472 by 7:10am ET; ETH down 2.8% from Monday's open, per Yahoo Finance.
Total crypto market cap fell 2.3% to $2.65T as Strait of Hormuz closure kept Brent above $104 a barrel.
CoinDesk pegs the 24-hour BTC range at $74,182-$78,290 with bulls needing a reclaim of $80,000 to invalidate the pullback.

stories.headline: Four central banks set rates this week as Brent above $104 keeps inflation hot
stories.tldr: The BOJ, Fed, ECB and BOE all decide policy this week into a $104 oil tape, the most concentrated rate-setting cluster of 2026.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Crypto's near-perfect inverse correlation with the dollar is at a four-year extreme, so any hawkish surprise lands directly on BTC's $76K bid.
the_details: BOJ, Federal Reserve, ECB and Bank of England all release decisions within the same trading week per CoinDesk's Crypto Week Ahead.
Brent crude held above $104 and WTI above $90 as the Iran conflict keeps Strait of Hormuz supply offline.
BTC and the DXY are moving in the most extreme opposition since 2022, raising the stakes on every central bank tone.

stories.headline: DOJ's Blanche tells crypto devs under probe: keep the line open or pay later
stories.tldr: Deputy AG Todd Blanche urged crypto developers under DOJ investigation to maintain active communication with prosecutors, signaling room for charging leniency.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The guidance hardens a post-Tornado Cash thaw and tells founders that silence, not the code, is now the trigger for max-charge outcomes.
the_details: Blanche addressed developers directly, framing cooperation as the dividing line between civil resolution and indictment.
Comes days after the SEC's April 13 'Covered User Interface' staff statement carved out front-end builders from broker-dealer registration.
Pairs with the SEC-CFTC March 17 joint release whose five-category token taxonomy is now the operative federal framework.

stories.headline: Solana ETF inflows crater 92% to $34M as SOL stalls at $83
stories.tldr: Spot Solana ETF inflows collapsed from $419M in November 2025 to just $34M in April 2026 as SOL trades 32% below its 2025 high.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: ETF demand was the bull-case bridge between Firedancer hype and price; without it, $52 becomes the next technical magnet.
the_details: Spot SOL ETF inflows fell from $419M (Nov 2025) to $34M (Apr 2026), a 92% drawdown per cryptopolitan data.
SOL trades at $83.17 with a $47.9B market cap, 32% off its 2025 YTD high.
Analysts target $52 if SOL fails to reclaim $86; Firedancer upgrade still on track for 1M TPS in 2026.

stories.headline: Solana flips Ethereum on weekly dApp revenue for fifth straight week
stories.tldr: Solana out-earned Ethereum on weekly decentralized application revenue for the fifth consecutive week, per CoinDesk tracking.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Sustained revenue dominance, not TVL, is the metric ETH bulls can't easily explain away — and it lands while spot SOL ETFs are bleeding.
the_details: Fifth consecutive weekly win on dApp revenue, a streak not seen since the 2024 memecoin peak.
Comes as Alpenglow upgrade has cut transaction finality below 200ms on mainnet.
Major issuers have additional spot SOL products in the filing pipeline despite the inflow slump.
